intro.speaker: Varn
intro.text: You walk these halls as though they belong to you. They do not. Nothing here belongs to anyone anymore.

responses.text: I've cleared the floor. Every last one.

task_complete.speaker: Varn
task_complete.text: I can feel it. The pressure in the chains has eased. That is not nothing — it is a kind of proof. I will remember that you followed through. Go deeper. I will find you when the time is right.

task_in_progress.speaker: Varn
task_in_progress.text: There are still things moving out there — I can feel the chains straining. Finish the work. Return when the floor is silent.

who_are_you.speaker: Varn
who_are_you.text: I am Varn. I was charged with keeping the chains of this place intact — the order that holds the dungeon's madness from consuming everything within it. The chains have frayed. I am less than I was.

responses.text: That sounds like a heavy burden.

passing_through.speaker: Varn
passing_through.text: Everyone says that. Then the dungeon takes them. You are the first I have seen who moves with purpose. That is worth something.

responses.text: What do you want from me?

the_chains.speaker: Varn
the_chains.text: Structure. Agreements between things that they will not unmake each other. The dungeon was once bound by such agreements. Something has undone them, and the chaos bleeds through every wall. I hold what little remains.

responses.text: And what does that cost you?
What do you need from me?

burden.speaker: Varn
burden.text: All necessary things are burdens. I do not resent it. Without someone to hold the structure, these halls would collapse into pure chaos. You would not survive that. Neither would I.

the_cost.speaker: Varn
the_cost.text: I cannot leave this post. The chains that bind the dungeon also bind me to it. A fitting irony. The Chainkeeper, chained.

what_do_you_want.speaker: Varn
what_do_you_want.text: Clear the corrupted things that wander near this post. Every creature that goes unchecked frays the chains further. I cannot leave to do it myself. Clear the floor entirely — do not leave anything standing.

responses.text: I'm not here to serve your agenda.

agree.speaker: Varn
agree.text: Good. We understand each other. Return when the floor is silent — I will know when it is done.

disagree.speaker: Varn
disagree.text: Then you are a wanderer like all the others. Perhaps you will reconsider when you see what the chaos does to those who ignore it. I will remember that too.
